headings.rules: Never use Inter or generic sans for headings. Apply tracking-tight for sizes above 32px.

colors.contrast_rules: Always check APCA. Text over images must have text-shadow. Explicit hover states (e.g. hover:bg-[#D48464] hover:text-white).

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Subtle roundness (max 8px). Flat Solid surfaces with 1px border. No Glassmorphism for heavy data.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise overlay (bg-noise) on the main background to kill digital flatness.
visual_enhancers.borders: Soft inner borders (border-white/10). No pure #000000 or #808080.

grids.dashboard: Mode B (High Density Grid): grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Tight gaps (gap-6).
grids.roster: Bento Grid layout with generous padding (p-6, p-8) around student cards.

layouts.general: Mobile-first responsive. Optical alignment over geometrical. Asymmetry where possible.
layouts.navigation: Visible sidebar or top nav with solid background (e.g. bg-[#1A1816]). No hamburger menus on desktop. NEVER use complete transparent background in header/overlays.
layouts.spacing_scale: Use 2-3x more spacing. Containers with borders MUST have internal padding.

motion.interactions: Hover states (slight lift -translate-y-1, shadow-lg, border darken).
motion.technical: Use specific transition properties. NEVER use 'transition: all'.

accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute.

media.icons: Use @phosphor-icons/react for high-end, elegant duotone weight icons.

3d_libraries.strategy: Minimal in dashboard. Focus on clean Recharts implementations with customized earthy colors.

image_urls.description: Silhouette of woman making yoga pose (warm, elegant)
Artistic portrait of a dancer with flowing orange fabric
Man with beard against a light background
A young woman with curly hair
Portrait of a woman in a white turtleneck

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a dashboard for a single dance teacher managing ~10 students. The vibe must be 'Earthy Editorial'—elegant, warm, and structured.
Do not use generic SaaS styling. Ditch the blues and teals. Use warm charcoal, terracotta, and soft sand.
Use Javascript (.jsx/.js files, NOT .tsx) for all components.
Install and use 'lucide-react' for general icons or '@phosphor-icons/react' for a premium duotone feel.
Use Shadcn/UI for modals, forms, dropdowns, and date pickers. Customize them to remove the default Vercel look (e.g., replace heavy shadows with 1px inner borders).
Ensure the layout has a clear navigation (e.g., sidebar) that never uses a transparent background. Use #1A1816.
Implement a roster table/grid with photos (use provided avatar URLs), hourly rates, and class logs.
Ensure 'data-testid' is present on all actionable items and key data displays (e.g., 'data-testid="student-card"').
Don't build generic centered layouts. Add tension. Left-align text inputs and tabular data.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Avoid generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Do not create the 'AI slop' aesthetic.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects carefully; prefer solid surfaces with delicate borders for this app.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This breaks transforms. Target specific properties.
You MUST NOT center align the app container. No '.App { text-align: center; }'.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp buttons with interaction animations.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
Shadcn components have opinionated rules, override them when necessary (e.g. height on tabs).
Use sonner for toasts, located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.jsx.
